_note: Add quiz sets here. Each set has an id, title, topic, and questions array.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly creates a tuple with a single element?
Tuples are mutable — you can change their elements after creation.

What is the output of:
```
t = (10, 20, 30, 40, 50)
print(t[1:4])
```

Complete the code to unpack only the first two elements and collect the rest:
```
a, b, ___rest = (1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
```
Which method returns the number of times a value appears in a tuple?

What does this print?
```
t = (5, 3, 8, 1)
print(sorted(t))
```
You can use a tuple as a dictionary key in Python.

What function converts a list into a tuple?
```
my_list = [1, 2, 3]
my_tuple = ___(my_list)
```
Which operator gives you the intersection of two sets?
Sets can contain duplicate elements.
Which method removes an element from a set without raising an error if it doesn't exist?
Which method adds an element to the end of a list?

What is the output?
```
nums = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5]
nums.sort()
print(nums)
```
The `pop()` method with no arguments removes the last element of a list.

Complete the list comprehension to get even numbers from 1–10:
```
evens = [x for x in range(1, 11) if x % 2 ___ 0]
```
What does `list.copy()` return?
choices: A reference to the same list
A shallow copy of the list
A deep copy of the list
A tuple version of the list
questions.answer: A shallow copy of the list

questions.question:
What is the output?
```
a = [1, 2, 3]
b = a
b.append(4)
print(a)
```
`reverse()` returns a new reversed list.

What method finds the position of an element in a list?
```
fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']
print(fruits.___('banana'))
```
